chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail Appln. No. S – 726 of 2024 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of bail application 1. For orders on office objection at flag ‘A’ 2. For hearing of post-arrest bail application. 15.11.2024 Mr. Achar Khan Gabol, Advocate for the Applicant Syed Sardar Ali Shah Rizvi, Additional PG for the State O R D E R After arguing the matter at some length, learned counsel for the applicant submits that he would be satisfied and will not press the instant bail application, if the learned trial Court is directed to conclude the trial in a shortest period of time. His such request has not been opposed by learned Additional PG for the State. In view of such proposal, this bail application is disposed of as not pressed. However, the trial Court is directed to conclude the trial within a period of forty five days, positively. No adjournment be granted to the applicant. However, for procuring the attendance of prosecution witnesses, the trial Court may issue coercive process against them. If the directions have not been complied with within the stipulated period, the applicant would be at liberty to move fresh application for bail before the trial Court, which will be decided in accordance with the law. Judge ARBROHI
